title: teucrium luteum subsp. contortostylum
intro: Teucrium luteum subsp. contortostylum, also known as Greek germander, is a plant species within the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and shrub-like appearance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective effects. Greek germander has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing liver damage, improving cognitive function, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
